description:
Advances: specled.realized_by

Deliverable:
Wire bare-module union hashing into both detectors. ApiBoundary.check_binding/3 and Implementation.check_subject/5 (or equivalent) recognize bare-module entries and call Canonical.hash_module_head_union/2 or hash_module_full_union/2 respectively. Add inferred? boolean field to the binding_ref shape (default false). ApiBoundary.check_binding/3's {:error, :not_found, ...} arm consults inferred? and returns [] when inferred? is true. Implementation tier is unchanged for inferred? — it still emits its own dangling. The flag does NOT appear on any finding map.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: api_boundary_test.exs and implementation_test.exs cover (a) bare-module entry resolves to Canonical hash; (b) inferred?: true + :not_found returns []; (c) inferred?: false + :not_found returns the dangling; (d) property test asserting no finding contains :inferred? or 'inferred?'.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: spec requirements specled.realized_by.bare_module_api_boundary_hash (detector wiring), bare_module_implementation_hash (detector wiring), implication_dangling_once (suppression), binding_ref_inferred_no_leak.

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator.ex (S5)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/effective_binding.ex (S2)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/canonical.ex (S3)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/closure.ex (no closure walk from bare modules)
- .spec/**
Allowed touches:
- lib/specled_ex/realization/api_boundary.ex
- lib/specled_ex/realization/implementation.ex
- test/specled_ex/realization/api_boundary_test.exs
- test/specled_ex/realization/implementation_test.exs

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT seed the closure walker from bare-module entries.
- Do NOT add inferred?-related logic to the implementation tier (the impl tier's own dangling is the surviving finding).
- Do NOT change the public shape of finding maps. The inferred? flag is orchestrator-internal only.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

  - lib/specled_ex/realization/api_boundary.ex
  - lib/specled_ex/realization/implementation.ex

Tests added:
  - api_boundary_test.exs: "run/3 — bare-module entries hash via Canonical.hash_module_head_union/2"
    * "no drift finding when committed bare-module hash matches current head-union"
    * "drift finding fires when committed bare-module hash differs"
  - api_boundary_test.exs: "run/3 — inferred? suppresses dangling, never leaks into findings"
    * "inferred?: true + :not_found → returns []"
    * "inferred?: false + :not_found → returns the dangling finding"
    * "absent inferred? key is treated as false (default behavior unchanged)"
    * "inferred?: true + bare-module not loadable → returns []"
    * "no finding map produced by run/3 contains an :inferred? or \"inferred?\" key"
  - implementation_test.exs: "run/4 — bare-module entries hash via Canonical.hash_module_full_union/2"
    * "no drift when committed bare-module hash matches current full-union"
    * "drift fires when committed bare-module hash differs"
    * "bare-module bindings do NOT seed the closure walk (no MFA → no subject hash)"
    * "MFA bindings and bare-module bindings coexist in the same subject"
  - implementation_test.exs: "run/4 — bare module not loadable → dangling"
    * "emits branch_guard_dangling_binding when the bare module fails to load"
  - implementation_test.exs: "run/4 — inferred? never leaks into finding maps"
    * "implementation findings never carry :inferred? or \"inferred?\" keys"

Verification stubs flipped: none in S4 (`.spec/**` is Out of Scope). Stub
flips for the requirements I covered will land alongside the orchestrator
wiring in S5 / final integration.

Summary:
ApiBoundary.run/3 now routes bare-module entries through
Canonical.hash_module_head_union/2 and consults the optional
binding_ref.inferred? flag to suppress dangling findings on inferred
entries, leaving the implementation tier as the single emitter.
Implementation.run/4 partitions impl_bindings into MFA-form (closure
walk, unchanged) and bare-module-form (per-module
hash_module_full_union/2, no closure seed). The inferred? flag is
orchestrator-internal — property tests on both detectors enforce that
no finding map ever carries an :inferred? or "inferred?" key. The S5
orchestrator wiring (expand_implications + inferred? marking + silent
seed) is the next ticket.

  - mix test test/specled_ex/realization/api_boundary_test.exs
    test/specled_ex/realization/implementation_test.exs → 31 tests, 0 failures

    all remaining are S5/S6/S7 territory (silent_seed,
    redundant_dup_warning, dedup_check_shared_seam, dedup_realized_by
    mix task, plus implication-drift requirements that need orchestrator
    wiring)

Scope check (mechanical):

  - 0 files matching Out of Scope (.spec/state.json regenerated by

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 0 (no `.spec/**` flips this stage; verification stubs for the S4 requirements stay `execute: false` until S5 orchestrator wiring lands)
- Requirements with passing tests: 6/6 relevant must-requirements
  - bare_module_api_boundary_hash → api_boundary_test.exs "run/3 — bare-module entries hash via Canonical.hash_module_head_union/2" (drift match + drift mismatch)
  - bare_module_implementation_hash → implementation_test.exs "run/4 — bare-module entries hash via Canonical.hash_module_full_union/2" (drift match + drift mismatch + mixed mfa+bare)
  - bare_module_no_closure_walk → implementation_test.exs "bare-module bindings do NOT seed the closure walk (no MFA → no subject hash)"
  - bare_module_runtime_only_discovery → implementation_test.exs "emits branch_guard_dangling_binding when the bare module fails to load"
  - implication_dangling_once → api_boundary_test.exs four cases (inferred?: true/false × not_found/not_loadable, plus absent-key default)
  - binding_ref_inferred_no_leak → property tests in BOTH api_boundary_test.exs and implementation_test.exs walk all four finding emission paths each
- Intent-level scope concerns: none
  - Closure walker NOT seeded from bare-module entries: Implementation.check_subject calls partition_bare_modules first; only `mfa_subject` (with bare modules stripped) is fed to compute_hash → Closure.compute. Confirmed at lib/specled_ex/realization/implementation.ex:115-145.
  - inferred? logic NOT added to implementation tier: `grep inferred lib/specled_ex/realization/implementation.ex` shows only @typedoc/@type references — no Map.get(:inferred?), no conditional. Implementation always emits its own dangling regardless of the flag.
  - inferred? does NOT appear on any finding map: property tests run mixed-path scenarios in both detectors; manual audit confirms no finding constructor (drift_finding, dangling_finding, bare_module_drift_finding, bare_module_dangling, umbrella detector_unavailable, the 4 ApiBoundary inline finding maps) writes the key.
- Project verification: pass (706 tests, 2 properties, 0 failures)
  - mix test → 706 tests, 2 properties, 0 failures
  - mix test test/specled_ex/realization/api_boundary_test.exs test/specled_ex/realization/implementation_test.exs → 31 tests, 0 failures
  - mix spec.check → 21 findings, all S5/S6/S7 territory (silent_seed, silent_seed_uses_merge, redundant_dup_warning, dedup_check_shared_seam, implication_drift_both_tiers, implication_amplification_dedup, mix_tasks dedup_realized_by_*). Matches the implementer's reported baseline; not a regression for S4.

Source-claim trace (functions on the live call path, not just imported):
- Canonical.hash_module_head_union/2 → ApiBoundary.check_binding/3 routes `{:ok, {:module, mod}}` to check_bare_module/3, which calls `Canonical.hash_module_head_union(mod)` directly (lib/specled_ex/realization/api_boundary.ex:97-100, 157-158).
- Canonical.hash_module_full_union/2 → Implementation.check_subject calls partition_bare_modules → check_bare_modules → check_bare_module, which calls `Canonical.hash_module_full_union(mod)` directly (lib/specled_ex/realization/implementation.ex:179-204).

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- mix spec.check regenerated .spec/state.json into the worktree as expected for this baseline; I did not revert it (per "Do not fix anything"). The committed diff vs base ref still touches only the 4 Allowed files.
- Verification stubs for the S4 requirements remain `execute: false` in realized_by.spec.md by design — they will flip alongside S5 orchestrator wiring per the implementer's note.
- The property test design relies on running enough path-covering scenarios to enforce the no-leak invariant in practice; if a future tier added a new finding constructor it would need the same audit. Worth keeping in mind for S5.
